title: fraxinus longicuspis var. pilosella
intro: Fraxinus longicuspis var. pilosella, also known as Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree native to Asia and is classified as a variety of the Fraxinus longicuspis species. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus longicuspis var. pilosella include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of the tree are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
